title: VPN Unlimited Review 2026: Is the Lifetime Deal Worth It?
metaDescription: Our in-depth VPN Unlimited review for 2026. We tested speeds, streaming, security, and the famous lifetime plan. Find out if KeepSolid's VPN is worth your money.
pros: Lifetime subscription plan available — one-time payment, no renewals
KeepSolid Wise stealth protocol bypasses VPN blocks in restrictive networks
Strong streaming support — unblocks Netflix US, Disney+, BBC iPlayer, Amazon Prime
Six VPN protocols including WireGuard, OpenVPN, IKEv2, and KeepSolid Wise
MonoDefense security bundle adds password manager, DNS firewall, and SmartDNS
Available on all major platforms including Linux and browser extensions
Dedicated static IP addresses available as an add-on
cons: US jurisdiction (Five Eyes member) — serious privacy concern
No independent security audit of no-logs claims
Collects IP addresses, device names, OS info, and session data during connections
Privacy policy allows data sharing with authorities and third parties
Very slow speeds on most protocols — only WireGuard delivers acceptable performance
Only 5 simultaneous device connections on standard plans
Small server network (500-3,000 servers depending on source) compared to major competitors
Torrenting restricted to only 3-5 designated servers

testResults.latency: 18ms on nearby servers, up to 210ms on distant servers
testResults.noLogsClaim: Claimed but NOT verified by any independent audit

speedLocal.note: Tested on nearest server from 283 Mbps baseline

pricing.freeTrial: 7-day free trial available on mobile apps

reviewContent:
## Our Verdict VPN Unlimited earns a **3.2 out of 5** rating in our 2026 review.
Pro Tip: The lifetime plan sounds appealing, but make sure you understand the trade-offs. See our best VPN guide for vetted alternatives with audited privacy at competitive long-term prices.
This is a VPN with one genuinely compelling feature — a lifetime subscription plan — wrapped in a package that raises too many privacy red flags to recommend for security-conscious users. The KeepSolid Wise stealth protocol is clever technology for bypassing VPN blocks, and streaming support is surprisingly strong. But the US jurisdiction, lack of independent audits, and aggressive data collection practices undermine the core purpose of using a VPN in the first place. If you are looking for a cheap, long-term VPN primarily for streaming and basic privacy from your ISP, VPN Unlimited's lifetime plan could save you money over the years. But if you care about genuine privacy and verified security, NordVPN and Surfshark offer far more trustworthy alternatives at competitive long-term prices. ## Who Is VPN Unlimited For?
💡 Pro Tip: VPN Unlimited's lifetime deal (~$49.99 one-time) appears on StackSocial and similar deal sites regularly. While it sounds great, lifetime VPN plans carry inherent risks — services can be discontinued, quality can decline, or companies can pivot. Before buying, compare to Surfshark's 2-year plan (~$52 total) from an audited, established provider. The extra cost buys you accountability and longevity guarantees.
VPN Unlimited makes sense for a specific type of user: - **budget-conscious streamers** who want a one-time payment VPN that unblocks Netflix, Disney+, and BBC iPlayer without ongoing subscription costs - **Users in restrictive networks** who need to bypass VPN blocks at schools, offices, or in countries like China and the UAE thanks to the KeepSolid Wise protocol - **Casual VPN users** who want basic ISP-level privacy without paying monthly fees - **KeepSolid ecosystem users** who already use MonoDefense tools like Passwarden or DNS Firewall and want everything bundled together ## Who Should NOT Use VPN Unlimited? - **Privacy-focused users**: The US jurisdiction, no independent audits, and data collection practices make VPN Unlimited a poor choice for anyone who needs genuine privacy protection - **Power users who need speed**: Speeds drop significantly on most protocols, and even WireGuard performance lags behind NordVPN and Surfshark - **Heavy torrenters**: P2P traffic is restricted to only 3-5 designated servers, limiting your options significantly - **Large households**: The 5-device limit on standard plans is restrictive — Surfshark offers unlimited connections for less money per month - **Journalists or activists**: The data collection practices and Five Eyes jurisdiction make this VPN inappropriate for anyone whose safety depends on anonymity ## Speed Test Results VPN Unlimited's speed performance is one of its weakest areas. The results vary dramatically depending on which protocol you use, and only WireGuard delivers speeds that could be considered acceptable for a modern VPN.
📖 Keep Reading: Check our Best VPNs of 2026 to see how the top providers rank, or read our VPN protocols guide to understand the technology behind your connection.
### Nearby Server Performance Using WireGuard on a nearby server, we recorded download speeds of **150 Mbps** and upload speeds of **77 Mbps** from a 283 Mbps baseline connection. That represents a **44% download speed loss** and a **74% upload speed loss**. These numbers are mediocre compared to NordVPN (which typically retains 85-90% of baseline speeds) and Surfshark (80-85% retention). Switching to OpenVPN or IKEv2 made things significantly worse. Upload speeds dropped to roughly 10% of baseline on non-WireGuard protocols, making them essentially unsuitable for video calls, large uploads, or any upload-intensive task. ### Long-Distance Performance Long-distance speeds are where VPN Unlimited truly struggles. Connecting from Europe to US servers, speeds dropped to around **58 Mbps** — a 79% reduction from baseline. Testing across multiple regions produced consistently disappointing results: | Server Location | Download | Upload | Ping | Speed Loss | |----------------|----------|--------|------|------------| | Nearest server | 150 Mbps | 77 Mbps | 18ms | 44% | | US East Coast | 58 Mbps | 31 Mbps | 95ms | 79% | | UK London | 89 Mbps | 42 Mbps | 68ms | 69% | | Singapore | 45 Mbps | 22 Mbps | 178ms | 84% | | Australia | 38 Mbps | 19 Mbps | 210ms | 87% | Security.org's testing found similar patterns, noting that Mac performance was significantly better than Windows, where latency spikes of over 6,000% were recorded — making the Windows client unsuitable for gaming or real-time applications. VPNMentor rated VPN Unlimited's speeds as "some of the worst" they had tested, recording only 12.43 Mbps on US servers from a Singapore baseline — a 96% speed drop. While our results were somewhat better, VPN Unlimited consistently places in the bottom tier for speed among the VPNs we have tested. ### Protocol Impact on Speed The protocol you choose makes an enormous difference with VPN Unlimited: - **WireGuard**: Best performance, roughly 50-56% speed retention nearby - **IKEv2**: Moderate download speeds but severely reduced uploads - **OpenVPN**: Slowest option, significant speed reduction across the board - **KeepSolid Wise**: Designed for bypassing blocks rather than speed — expect additional overhead Our recommendation: always use WireGuard unless you specifically need KeepSolid Wise for bypassing network restrictions. ## Security & Privacy This is VPN Unlimited's most problematic area, and the primary reason for its low rating. For a product that markets itself as a privacy tool, the security posture raises serious concerns. ### US Jurisdiction — Five Eyes Member VPN Unlimited is operated by KeepSolid Inc., headquartered at 347 5th Avenue, Suite 1402-419, New York, NY 10016. The United States is a founding member of the Five Eyes intelligence-sharing alliance, which means US authorities can legally compel companies to hand over user data — and in some cases, prevent them from disclosing that they have done so through gag orders. This is a fundamental problem for a VPN provider. While KeepSolid claims a "zero-logs" policy, the US legal framework means the company could theoretically be forced to start logging without informing users. Compare this to Mullvad (Sweden, with proven no-data-to-seize in a police raid) or ProtonVPN (Switzerland, outside Five Eyes entirely). ### No Independent Audits As of March 2026, VPN Unlimited has **never undergone an independent security audit**
Pro Tip: No independent audit means no verified privacy. Our VPN privacy guide explains what third-party audits check and which VPNs have the strongest audit track records.
to verify its no-logs claims. This is a glaring omission in an industry where independent verification has become the standard. NordVPN has completed six no-logs audits (by PwC and Deloitte). Surfshark has been audited by Deloitte. ExpressVPN, ProtonVPN, and even smaller providers like Mullvad and IVPN have all submitted to independent verification. Without an audit, VPN Unlimited's no-logs claim is nothing more than a marketing statement. Users must trust KeepSolid's word alone — and the privacy policy gives reasons to be skeptical. ### Data Collection Concerns VPN Unlimited's privacy policy reveals that the company collects more data than its "zero-logs" marketing suggests: - **IP addresses** are recorded during active sessions - **Operating system, browser type, and device names** are collected - **Session dates and traffic amounts** are logged - **Personal information is retained for a minimum of 7 years** per their privacy policy - The policy states they "may also collect personal data from third parties" - They reserve the right to share information "with authorities or legitimate third parties" Experte.com's review noted that VPN Unlimited's "biggest shortcomings" are "in terms of privacy," and the claimed zero-logs policy is "compromised by retention of IP addresses, OS details, and device names during active sessions." AllAboutCookies.org described VPN Unlimited as having "worrying data collection policies" and gave it a poor privacy score due to these practices. ### Encryption and Protocols On the positive side, VPN Unlimited uses **AES-256 encryption** — the same standard used by governments and military organizations. The service offers six protocols: 1. **WireGuard** — Modern, fast, lean codebase 2. **OpenVPN (TCP/UDP)** — Industry standard, battle-tested 3. **IKEv2** — Good for mobile connections 4. **L2TP/IPSec** — Legacy protocol, not recommended 5. **KeepSolid Wise TCP** — Proprietary stealth protocol 6. **KeepSolid Wise UDP** — Faster variant of Wise protocol The variety of protocols is actually a strength — particularly the KeepSolid Wise options for users in restrictive environments. ### Kill Switch VPN Unlimited includes a kill switch on **Android 8+, Windows, macOS, and iOS**. However, the kill switch is **not available on Linux** — a notable gap. Our testing confirmed the kill switch works as expected on supported platforms, blocking all traffic when the VPN connection drops. DNS leak tests were clean: **no DNS, IPv6, or WebRTC leaks** were detected during our testing, which is a positive finding. ### KeepSolid Wise Protocol — The Standout Feature The KeepSolid Wise protocol is VPN Unlimited's most innovative security feature. It disguises VPN traffic as regular HTTPS traffic using TCP port 443 and UDP port 33434, making it extremely difficult for network administrators or government firewalls to detect and block. Wise comes in three variants: - **Wise UDP**: Fastest option, good for general use behind blocks - **Wise TCP**: More reliable in heavily restricted networks - **Wise TLS**: Newest variant combining speed with the strongest stealth capabilities The protocol uses OpenVPN as its backbone with 256-bit encryption. For users in China, the UAE, Iran, or anyone dealing with corporate or school network restrictions, KeepSolid Wise is a genuinely useful tool. It is one of the few legitimate reasons to consider VPN Unlimited over competitors. ## Streaming Performance Streaming is arguably VPN Unlimited's strongest category and the most compelling reason to consider the service. Despite its other shortcomings, VPN Unlimited performs surprisingly well at unblocking geo-restricted content. ### Netflix VPN Unlimited successfully **unblocked Netflix US** in our testing, along with several other Netflix regional libraries. The service maintains dedicated streaming-optimized servers that are regularly updated to stay ahead of Netflix's VPN detection. VPNMentor rated VPN Unlimited's streaming capabilities at 8.8 out of 10 — "on the higher end when it comes to streaming VPNs." However, a caveat: the slow speeds mean that 4K streaming may buffer on distant servers. We experienced smooth 1080p playback on US servers but occasional buffering when attempting 4K content. ### Other Streaming Platforms | Platform | Works? | Notes | |----------|--------|-------| | Netflix US | Yes | Unblocked consistently with optimized servers | | Disney+ | Yes | Works on US and UK servers | | BBC iPlayer | Yes | UK servers unblock reliably | | Amazon Prime Video | Yes | US library accessible | | Hulu | Yes | US servers work | | HBO Max | Yes | Consistent access | | ESPN+ | Yes | Sports streaming works | | Fubo TV | No | Only platform that consistently failed | Experte.com confirmed similar results, noting successful streaming across "Netflix, Amazon Prime Video, Disney+, and BBC iPlayer" with servers specifically optimized for each platform. ### SmartDNS VPN Unlimited includes **SmartDNS** functionality as part of the MonoDefense bundle. SmartDNS routes only DNS queries through VPN Unlimited's servers, allowing streaming devices that do not support VPN apps (like certain smart TVs and gaming consoles) to access geo-restricted content without the speed overhead of full VPN encryption. ## Pricing & Plans VPN Unlimited's pricing structure is straightforward, with the lifetime plan being the obvious headline feature. ### Current Pricing (March 2026) | Plan | Monthly Cost | Total Cost | Billing | Devices | |------|-------------|------------|---------|----------| | Monthly | **$9.99/mo** | $9.99 | Monthly | 5 | | 1 Year | **$5.00/mo** | $59.99 | Annually | 5 | | 3 Years | **$4.50/mo** | $161.99 | One-time | 5 | | Lifetime | **One-time** | $199.99 | Never | 5 | | Monthly (10 devices) | **$14.99/mo** | $14.99 | Monthly | 10 | | 1 Year (10 devices) | **$7.50/mo** | $89.99 | Annually | 10 | | Lifetime (10 devices) | **One-time** | $299.99 | Never | 10 | ### The Lifetime Plan — Is It Worth It? The lifetime subscription at $199.99 is VPN Unlimited's signature offering. At the annual plan rate of $59.99 per year, the lifetime plan pays for itself in approximately **3.3 years**. If VPN Unlimited continues operating for 5+ years, it becomes genuinely cheaper than any competitor. But there is a critical question: **will KeepSolid still be around in 10 years?** Lifetime VPN deals carry inherent risk. The company must continue generating enough revenue to maintain servers, develop apps, and update security — indefinitely. Several VPN providers who offered lifetime deals have either degraded their service quality over time or shut down entirely. KeepSolid has been operating since 2013 and has expanded into a broader security suite (MonoDefense), which suggests some business stability. However, the lifetime plan is still a gamble. If privacy and long-term reliability are your priorities, a well-established provider with transparent recurring pricing (like NordVPN or Mullvad) is a safer bet. Third-party retailers like StackSocial occasionally offer the lifetime plan at steep discounts — sometimes as low as $69.99. These deals may be worth considering if you want to minimize your financial risk. ### MonoDefense Bundle For an additional $2.00 per month, VPN Unlimited can be upgraded to the **MonoDefense** security bundle, which includes: - **VPN Unlimited** — the VPN service - **Passwarden** — password manager - **DNS Firewall** — DNS-level ad and malware blocking - **SmartDNS** — streaming DNS routing - **Authenticator** — two-factor authentication app (included free)
💡 Pro Tip: VPN Unlimited's Personal Server add-on gives you a dedicated IP address that only you use. This virtually eliminates the risk of streaming services or websites blocking you due to shared VPN IP abuse. Add it during checkout or in your KeepSolid account dashboard. If you use VPN Unlimited primarily for streaming, a dedicated IP is worth the extra cost for consistent unblocking.
At $11.99/month total for the monthly bundle, this is competitive if you would otherwise pay for a separate password manager and VPN. However, none of the individual tools in MonoDefense are best-in-class in their respective categories. ### Money-Back Guarantee VPN Unlimited offers a **30-day money-back guarantee** on all plans, including the lifetime subscription. This is standard for the industry and matches what NordVPN, Surfshark, and ExpressVPN offer. A 7-day free trial is also available on mobile apps (iOS and Android). ### How It Compares on Price Over a 3-year period: - **VPN Unlimited Lifetime**: $199.99 total (one-time) - **NordVPN (2-year plan + renewal)**: ~$307 total - **Surfshark (2-year plan + renewal)**: ~$200 total - **Mullvad**: $198 total (flat rate) The lifetime plan looks attractive on paper — but you are trading privacy guarantees and audit verification for a lower price. That is a trade-off each user must evaluate for themselves. ## Apps & Usability VPN Unlimited provides native apps for all major platforms: - **Desktop**: Windows, macOS, Linux - **Mobile**: Android, iOS - **Browser extensions**: Chrome, Firefox, Edge - **Other**: Apple TV, Android TV, Fire TV Stick - **Router support**: Compatible via manual configuration The app interface is clean and functional, if somewhat dated compared to the polished designs of NordVPN or Surfshark. The main screen shows a connect button and server list, with protocol selection accessible through settings. ### Key Features - **Kill switch**: Available on Windows, macOS, iOS, Android 8+ (NOT Linux) - **Split tunneling**: Windows only - **Trusted networks**: Auto-connect when joining untrusted Wi-Fi networks - **DNS leak protection**: Enabled by default - **Dedicated static IP**: Available as a paid add-on ($14.99/year) - **Favorite servers**: Save frequently used server locations - **KeepSolid Wise**: One-click protocol switching for bypassing VPN blocks ### Device Limit The standard plan allows **5 simultaneous connections**. You can upgrade to 10 devices for an additional fee. This is below the industry average — NordVPN allows 10 connections, and Surfshark offers unlimited. For the price, 5 devices feels restrictive. ### Platform-Specific Issues Security.org's testing revealed significant performance differences between platforms. The **Windows client** showed severe latency issues (6,000%+ increase), making it unsuitable for gaming or video calls. The **macOS client** performed notably better. This platform inconsistency is concerning and suggests uneven development attention across platforms. Split tunneling is **only available on Windows**, which limits its usefulness for macOS and Linux users who may want to route specific apps outside the VPN tunnel. ## VPN Unlimited vs NordVPN vs Surfshark | Feature | VPN Unlimited | NordVPN | Surfshark | |---------|--------------|---------|----------| | Monthly price (best deal) | $4.50/mo (3-year) | $3.39/mo (2-year) | $2.49/mo (2-year) | | Lifetime plan | Yes ($199.99) | No | No | | Servers | ~3,000 | 7,100+ | 3,200+ | | Countries | 80 | 118 | 100 | | Simultaneous devices | 5 | 10 | Unlimited | | Independent audits | None | 6 (PwC, Deloitte) | 2 (Deloitte, Cure53) | | Netflix unblocking | Yes | Yes (30+ libraries) | Yes (30+ libraries) | | Kill switch | Yes (not Linux) | Yes (all platforms) | Yes (all platforms) | | Jurisdiction | US (Five Eyes) | Panama | Netherlands | | Stealth protocol | KeepSolid Wise | NordWhisper | NoBorders mode | | Speed retention | ~50% nearby | ~85-90% nearby | ~80-85% nearby | | Torrenting | 3-5 servers only | All servers | All servers | | Split tunneling | Windows only | Windows, Android, macOS | All platforms | The comparison table makes VPN Unlimited's weaknesses stark. It loses to both NordVPN and Surfshark on nearly every metric except one: the lifetime plan. If that single feature is your deciding factor and you accept the trade-offs, VPN Unlimited has a unique value proposition. Otherwise, NordVPN and Surfshark are objectively superior choices. ## What I Don't Like About VPN Unlimited ### 1. The Privacy Policy Contradicts the Marketing VPN Unlimited markets itself with a "zero-logs" badge, but the privacy policy tells a different story. Collecting IP addresses, device information, session timestamps, and traffic amounts during connections — and retaining personal information for 7 years — is not zero-logging. It is selective logging with a misleading label. Until KeepSolid submits to an independent audit, there is no way to verify what is actually happening with user data. ### 2. Speed Is Not Competitive In 2026, with NordVPN delivering 900+ Mbps and Surfshark consistently hitting 700+ Mbps, VPN Unlimited's 150 Mbps on nearby servers via WireGuard is simply not competitive. The non-WireGuard protocol speeds are even worse. For users with fast internet connections, VPN Unlimited becomes a significant bottleneck. ### 3. The Lifetime Plan Is a Double-Edged Sword The lifetime plan is the primary selling point, but it also raises sustainability questions. How does a company maintain infrastructure, pay developers, and update security for users who will never pay again? History shows that many VPN providers who offered lifetime deals eventually degraded service quality. KeepSolid has survived longer than most, but the fundamental business model tension remains. ### 4. Torrenting Restrictions Restricting P2P traffic to only 3-5 servers is a significant limitation. If those servers are slow or overloaded, torrenting performance suffers with no alternatives. NordVPN and Surfshark allow torrenting on all servers. ### 5. Platform Inconsistency The massive performance gap between macOS and Windows clients — particularly the 6,000% latency spike on Windows — suggests that the development team is not maintaining consistent quality across platforms. No kill switch on Linux is another gap that privacy-focused users will notice. ## Who Should Choose VPN Unlimited? **Choose VPN Unlimited if you:** - Want a one-time payment VPN with no recurring costs and primarily need it for streaming - Need to bypass VPN blocks in restrictive networks or countries using KeepSolid Wise - Already use KeepSolid's MonoDefense ecosystem and want everything in one bundle - Have a slow internet connection (under 100 Mbps) where the speed limitations will be less noticeable **Choose NordVPN instead if you:** - Want verified privacy with independently audited no-logs claims - Need fast speeds for gaming, 4K streaming, or large downloads - Want the most complete feature set and largest server network **Choose Surfshark instead if you:** - Have a large household and need unlimited simultaneous connections - Want the best value per month on a subscription plan - Need consistent cross-platform performance [Get VPN Unlimited from $4.50/mo](https://go.zerotovpn.com/vpn-unlimited) ## FAQ **Is VPN Unlimited safe to use in 2026?** VPN Unlimited uses AES-256 encryption and offers a kill switch on most platforms, which provides basic security. However, the service has never undergone an independent security audit, is based in the US (a Five Eyes country), and its privacy policy reveals collection of IP addresses and session data. For basic ISP-level privacy and streaming, it is adequate. For genuine anonymity or security-critical use cases, we recommend audited alternatives like NordVPN or ProtonVPN. **Does VPN Unlimited work with Netflix?** Yes. VPN Unlimited successfully unblocks Netflix US and several other Netflix regional libraries as of March 2026. The service maintains dedicated streaming-optimized servers. VPNMentor rated its streaming performance at 8.8 out of 10. However, the slow speeds may cause buffering on 4K content, especially on distant servers. **Is the VPN Unlimited lifetime plan worth it?** Financially, the $199.99 lifetime plan pays for itself in about 3.3 years compared to the annual plan. If KeepSolid continues operating for 5+ years, it is a good deal on paper. The risk is that lifetime VPN deals depend on the company's long-term viability. KeepSolid has been operating since 2013, which is a positive sign, but there are no guarantees. Third-party retailers sometimes offer the lifetime plan for as low as $69.99, which significantly reduces the financial risk. **Does VPN Unlimited keep logs?** VPN Unlimited claims a "zero-logs" policy, but this has never been independently verified. The privacy policy states that the company collects IP addresses, operating system information, device names, session dates, and traffic amounts during active connections. Personal information is retained for a minimum of 7 years. This level of data collection is significantly more than privacy-focused competitors like Mullvad, ProtonVPN, or NordVPN. **Can VPN Unlimited bypass VPN blocks in China?** Yes. The proprietary KeepSolid Wise protocol is specifically designed to disguise VPN traffic as regular HTTPS traffic, making it difficult for firewalls to detect and block. Wise uses TCP port 443 and UDP port 33434. It is available in three variants (UDP, TCP, and TLS) and works on iOS, Android, Windows, macOS, and Linux. While no VPN can guarantee 100% reliability in China, KeepSolid Wise is one of the more effective stealth protocols available. **How many devices can I use with VPN Unlimited?** The standard plan allows 5 simultaneous device connections. You can upgrade to 10 devices for an additional fee ($14.99/month or $89.99/year). This is below the industry average — NordVPN allows 10 devices and Surfshark offers unlimited connections. You can install the app on unlimited devices, but only 5 (or 10) can be connected at the same time. **What is KeepSolid Wise protocol?** KeepSolid Wise is VPN Unlimited's proprietary stealth protocol. It uses OpenVPN as its backbone with 256-bit encryption but disguises VPN traffic to look like regular HTTPS traffic. This makes it effective at bypassing VPN blocks in restrictive environments like China, Iran, UAE, and corporate or school networks. It comes in three variants: Wise UDP (fastest), Wise TCP (most reliable), and Wise TLS (newest, combining speed and stealth). **What is MonoDefense?** MonoDefense is KeepSolid's all-in-one security bundle that includes VPN Unlimited, Passwarden (password manager), DNS Firewall, SmartDNS (streaming DNS routing), and Authenticator (2FA app). It costs an additional $2.00/month on top of your VPN Unlimited subscription. While convenient, none of the individual tools are best-in-class in their respective categories. ## How VPN Unlimited Compares See how VPN Unlimited measures up in our head-to-head comparisons: - [**VPN Unlimited vs NordVPN**](/compare/vpn-unlimited-vs-nordvpn) — How does it stack up against the market leader? - [**VPN Unlimited vs Surfshark**](/compare/vpn-unlimited-vs-surfshark) — Value comparison with a top budget VPN ## Sources ## Related Reviews More VPN reviews you might find useful: - [**CactusVPN Review**](/reviews/cactusvpn) — Another affordable VPN with similar features - [**Speedify Review**](/reviews/speedify) — Unique channel bonding VPN at a similar tier - [**Goose VPN Review**](/reviews/goose-vpn) — Unlimited devices in the same price range
